title: Polyscias macgillivrayi
taxonomy: Polyscias macgillivrayi is classified within the family Araliaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the ginseng or ivy family. This family is part of the larger order Apiales, a group of flowering plants that also includes the carrot and parsley families. The genus Polyscias is a group of evergreen trees and shrubs native to the Pacific Islands and Australia, with Polyscias macgillivrayi being one of its species. The species Polyscias macgillivrayi is native to New Guinea, and it is also known as the MacGillivray's tree or MacGillivray's polynesian tree. Further classification of Polyscias macgillivrayi can be broken down into its kingdom (Plantae) and phylum (Angiospermophyta).

intro: Polyscias macgillivrayi, also known as the Australian houttuynia or mountain spinach, is a perennial herb native to Australia and New Guinea. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it useful for treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues.
